reason: The remediation only edits a single line in /etc/default/useradd to set the INACTIVE value, which is a standard STIG hardening step. It does not affect bootloader, kernel, or critical services, and the change is reversible. The risk of accidental lockout is low if the value is set appropriately.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential account lockout if INACTIVE is set to an unintended value; May affect automated account management scripts
